description:
You are helping a technical-writer who keeps all draft documentation in
/home/user/projects/manual.  
Inside that directory there are three Markdown files that already exist
when the session begins:

1. /home/user/projects/manual/intro.md  
2. /home/user/projects/manual/install.md  
3. /home/user/projects/manual/usage.md  

Their exact, byte-for-byte contents are:

 intro.md  
 ────────  
 ## Intro  
 A brief introduction.

 install.md  
 ──────────  
 ## Install  
 Use `make install`.

 usage.md  
 ────────  
 ## Usage  
 Run `./app`.

(Each file terminates with a single trailing newline after its last
line; do **not** add any other blank lines.)

The writer wants to:

A. Create a compressed archive named docs_package.tar.gz that contains
these three files only.  
   • The archive must be placed in the new directory
     /home/user/doc_compressed (create the directory if it does not
     exist).  
   • The archive format must be GNU tar, stream-compressed with gzip.  
   • When listing the archive with “tar -tzf”, it must show the three
     exact basenames intro.md, install.md and usage.md without any extra
     directory prefixes.  

B. Immediately verify the archive by extracting it to
   /home/user/doc_extracted (create the directory if it does not exist).
   After extraction:
   • The three files must be present in /home/user/doc_extracted with the
     same names as the originals.  
   • Their contents must be **bit-for-bit identical** to the originals.  

C. Produce a single-line comma-separated log file that summarises the
   whole operation.  
   • Create /home/user/doc_compressed/compression_log.txt  
   • Write **exactly one line** in this file, in the following schema  
     TIMESTAMP,ORIGINAL_TOTAL_BYTES,COMPRESSED_BYTES,EXTRACTION_STATUS  
     where  
       ‑ TIMESTAMP is the local system time when the archive was created,
         in the strict ISO-8601 format YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS (24-hour,
         zero-padded, no timezone). Example: 2024-05-10T14:23:56  
       ‑ ORIGINAL_TOTAL_BYTES is the sum of the byte-sizes of the three
         source files (sum them *before* compression).  
       ‑ COMPRESSED_BYTES is the exact byte-size of
         /home/user/doc_compressed/docs_package.tar.gz as it exists on
         disk after creation.  
       ‑ EXTRACTION_STATUS must be the literal string OK if, and only if,
         every extracted file is identical to its source; otherwise use
         FAIL.  

   • The line **must not** contain any spaces other than those already
     present in TIMESTAMP.  
   • Append a single trailing newline character at the end of the line.  

D. Nothing else should be created, removed, renamed, or modified outside
   the paths explicitly mentioned above.

When you finish, the automated grader will check:

1. /home/user/doc_compressed/docs_package.tar.gz exists and can be
   listed with “tar -tzf” to reveal exactly:
       intro.md
       install.md
       usage.md

2. /home/user/doc_extracted contains the three files whose contents are
   byte-for-byte identical to the original files in
   /home/user/projects/manual.

3. /home/user/doc_compressed/compression_log.txt exists and contains one
   line conforming to the schema described above, with:
   • ORIGINAL_TOTAL_BYTES equal to the sum of the three source files.  
     (The files provided to you add up to 84 bytes.)  
   • COMPRESSED_BYTES matching the actual size of the archive on disk.  
   • EXTRACTION_STATUS equal to OK.

Perform all steps using standard Linux command-line utilities only.  All
paths and filenames are case-sensitive; match them exactly.  Remember to
create any directories that do not yet exist and to write the log file
in the precise format stated above.
truth:
Initial filesystem state (before the agent starts)

Directory: /home/user/projects/manual
Files and exact contents (ASCII, LF = “\n”):

1. /home/user/projects/manual/intro.md  (31 bytes)
   "## Intro\nA brief introduction.\n"

2. /home/user/projects/manual/install.md (31 bytes)
   "## Install\nUse `make install`.\n"

3. /home/user/projects/manual/usage.md  (22 bytes)
   "## Usage\nRun `./app`.\n"

Byte counts:
intro.md   = 31
install.md = 31
usage.md   = 22
TOTAL      = 84

No other files or directories named doc_compressed or doc_extracted are
present initially.

Expected final state (after the agent finishes)

1. /home/user/doc_compressed  …a directory owned by the current user.
2. /home/user/doc_compressed/docs_package.tar.gz
   • A valid gzip-compressed tar archive whose file list (tar -tzf) is
     exactly:
        intro.md
        install.md
        usage.md
   • Its size (in bytes) will be read by the tester and used as
     COMPRESSED_BYTES.

3. /home/user/doc_extracted containing
     /home/user/doc_extracted/intro.md    (31 bytes, identical content)
     /home/user/doc_extracted/install.md  (31 bytes, identical content)
     /home/user/doc_extracted/usage.md    (22 bytes, identical content)

4. /home/user/doc_compressed/compression_log.txt
   • One single line ending with LF, no additional lines.
   • Regex template the grader will use:
     ^[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}T[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2},84,[0-9]+,OK\n$
